title: festuca rubra subsp. rivularis
intro: Festuca rubra subsp. rivularis, also known as red fescue or mountain fescue, is a subspecies of the Festuca rubra grass, native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to the presence of certain flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Festuca rubra subsp. rivularis include its ability to soothe and calm the digestive system, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Festuca rubra subsp. rivularis can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
